sglang.title: [Spec] Replace shared-infra dflash special-cases with capabilities (WAR barrier + seq_lens_cpu)
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt: Replace the hardcoded not spec_algorithm.is_dflash() WAR-barrier check in the scheduler with a needs_war_barrier worker capability (default True; DFLASH self-fences its shared req_to_token writes via verify_done / plan-stream deps, so it overrides to False). Behavior-identical refactor — the schedul

sglang.title: chore: bump sgl-deep-gemm build-time apache-tvm-ffi 0.1.9 -> 0.1.11
sglang.author: MartinHua
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
What
Bump TVM_FFI_VER in docker/sgl-deep-gemm.Dockerfile 0.1.9 → 0.1.11. This is the build-time apache-tvm-ffi that release-whl-deepgemm.yml installs to compile the sgl-deep-gemm wheel's _C.so against tvm-ffi's C++ ABI.
Runtime-safe on its own — it changes nothing in the current install; it only aff

sglang.title: dflash: drop verify_done barrier; rely on scheduler WAR fallback
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
DFLASH self-fenced its req_to_token writes with a verify_done event — a CPU-blocking cudaEventSynchronize (~2.1ms x 37/profile), the largest remaining sync in the dflash overlap loop.
Now that seq_lens CPU values relay through the FutureMap (publish_ready, #29232), verify_done's only rema

sglang.title: [diffusion] CI: make consistency GT probe robust to transient CDN failures
sglang.author: mickqian
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Problem
The diffusion consistency check's _remote_file_exists (in multimodal_gen/test/test_utils.py) can report a present GT as missing whenever raw.githubusercontent.com momentarily fails, surfacing as a misleading CI failure:
[consistency] GT not found for <case>. See logs for instructions to add

sglang.title: Fix FlashInfer A2A dispatcher during CUDA graph capture
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Skip the EP>1 runtime token count query branch during CUDA graph capture in the FlashInfer MoE A2A dispatcher.
The captured graph uses fixed geometry, so querying runtime token counts via NCCL all-gather during capture is incorrect.
Gate the branch on not is_graph_capture (combining get_is_

sglang.title: feat: first-class session identity in SGLang
sglang.author: ishandhanani
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Adds a scalar top-level session_id as stable request identity and wires it through native, Engine, OpenAI, Responses, and gRPC entry points. This keeps continual-prompt session_params unchanged while letting the existing session radix cache use session_id without requiring /open_session.
How This Wa

sglang.title: [scheduler] Add scheduler metrics reporter init hook
sglang.author: yinghai
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Route Scheduler metrics reporter construction through an overridable init hook.
Preserve the existing default SchedulerMetricsReporter behavior.
Allow subclasses to provide specialized reporter implementations without mutating module globals.

Testing

uv run python -m py_compile python/sgl

sglang.title: Fix dummy weight init for tensor subclasses
sglang.author: aurickq
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Fix initialize_dummy_weights() so tensor subclasses are classified by their logical dtype instead of their raw storage dtype.
Motivation
Some tensor subclasses expose low-bit storage through .data, while the wrapper tensor itself reports the logical dtype that its tensor operations support.

sglang.title: Fix DSA indexer fusion bug causing excessive memory consumption.
sglang.author: b8zhong
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
#29564 found this bug. It's actually from the incorrect creation of rope buffer per layer x context length. Now, it will be 0.25 gb/GPU.
on TP 4 GLM-5.2:
Fusion before fix: 2,019,520 tok/pool
With disable: 2,398,784 tok/pool
After this PR: 2,394,496 tok/pool
Thanks! @mmangkad


CI States
Latest PR T

sglang.title: [HiCache] Detect for double-free in HostKVCache
sglang.author: stepinto
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The uses of host_indices in HiRadixCache and HiCacheController are tricky, because of the flexible life-time.  This patch adds detection for double-free in HostKVCache.  When double-free happens, an assertion failure will occur.
Modifications
Modified HostKVCache, using a booelan tensor t

sglang.title: [dflash] fa3/fa4: device-side page table; drop seq_lens_cpu D2H sync
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Build the page table on-device via build_trtllm_mha_page_table (Triton kernel from #28106, self-guards per request on cache_seqlens) in the cuda-graph decode / draft-decode / target-verify paths — no host-side seq_lens_cpu.max().item() for page-table sizing
Set needs_cpu_seq_lens = not is_d

sglang.title: [CPU] enable fused_sigmoid_mul on CPU device
sglang.author: mingfeima
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Add a CPU kernel for attention output gating used by Qwen3.5: attn_output * sigmoid(gate).
Previously, the CPU path fell back to PyTorch eager (reshape + sigmoid + mul_), while GPU used a fused Triton kernel. This This PR adds fused_sigmoid_mul_cpu and wires it into Qwen3.5 on CPU.
Modifi

sglang.title: [misc] Use --cuda-graph-max-bs-decode in tests, examples, and docs
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Replace the deprecated --cuda-graph-max-bs alias with the canonical --cuda-graph-max-bs-decode across CI tests, examples, and docs to stop emitting the deprecation warning. The deprecation alias itself is left in place for external users.
Mechanical verification
This is a pure mechanical flag rename

sglang.title: [CPU] use faster exp in silu_and_mul
sglang.author: mingfeima
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
This PR speeds up the CPU silu_and_mul_cpu vector path by replacing exp() with exp_u20() in sgl-kernel/csrc/cpu/activation.cpp.
This matches the faster vector exponential approximation already used by other CPU kernels in sgl-kernel, while keeping the scalar fallback unchanged.
Modificati

sglang.title: [weight checker] refactor: add precision branch; allow ULP quant err; used chunked compare
sglang.author: yueming-yuan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The weight checker's compare/checksum actions require two block-quantized weights to be bit-identical after dequant. But two independent yet faithful quantizations of the same source weight — e.g. before vs. after a weight update that requantizes — can legitimately differ per element. Tol

sglang.title: [Spec] Publish DFLASH verify read-done event for fine-grained WAR barrier
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
What
Publish the WAR read_done event from the DFLASH target-verify forward so the scheduler's global _apply_war_barrier takes the fine-grained wait_event(read_done) fast path instead of the coarse wait_stream(forward_stream) fallback.
Why it is valid
The target-verify cuda graph builds its page tabl

sglang.title: [NPU][Bugfix] Fix a ModelSlim loading failure
sglang.author: stellaxcpeng
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Fix a ModelSlim loading failure on Ascend NPU for GLM-5.2 / DSA-style models.
Previously, ModelSlimConfig.get_linear_scheme() returned None for these layers, but get_quant_method() still returned ModelSlimLinearMethod. This caused model initialization to fail when ModelSlimLinearMethod.cr

Access MultimodalDataItem fields (feature, precomputed_embeddings, hash) directly since the

sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Per Lianmin's feedback (2026-06-19): make the default arguments just work — drop hand-set serve flags where the runtime default is correct or better, instead of overriding in the cookbook.
GLM-5.2 (zai-org/glm-5.2.jsx)

--mem-fraction-static: keep it explicit (0.85 / 0.8 / 0.9). Earlier in this PR t

sglang.title: [NPU] Qwen3-VL-30B use split_qkv_rmsnorm_rope for extend
sglang.author: silencejade
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

When using the torch_npu.npu_mrope interface with Qwen3-VL-30B, certain precision issues occur. We need to adjust the invocation path: during the extend phase, utilize the fused operator split_qkv_rmsnorm_rope to bypass this operator.
Modifications

Adjust the branch judgment so that for

vllm.title: [ROCm][Perf] Add Fused Shared Expert (FSE) support for GLM-4.5/6/7
vllm.author: omirosh
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Extend the AITER Fused Shared Expert (FSE) path — originally added for DeepSeek-V2/V3 (#28540) and Qwen3-Next (#39280) — to the GLM-4 MoE family (GLM-4.5, GLM-4.6, GLM-4.7). When VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_FUSION_SHARED_EXPERTS=1, the shared expert is folded into the AITER FusedMoE kernel as n_shar

Purpose
#42224 has supported ViT CUDA graph for Step3-VL. However, it has only considered mm inputs without generating local patches, which will lead to RuntimeError for larger images.

(EngineCore

vllm.title: [Hardware][AMD][CI] Tweak mirrored tests; improve CI base dependency change detection
vllm.author: mawong-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This PR makes the following changes in order to improve AMD CI stability:

Unmirrors the Distributed Tests (2xH100-2xMI300) test group, which was originally promoted in #45998. This test group was never passing but was believed to be due to a bug (that was recently fixed by vllm-project/ci-i

vllm.title: [Render][Speculator] Add return_loss_mask to render endpoint for training data generation
vllm.author: WindChimeRan
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Adds a return_loss_mask field to ChatCompletionRequest and a loss_mask field to GenerateRequest, so POST /v1/chat/completions/render can return a per-token assistant/trainable mask alongside token_ids. This is needed by downstream training frameworks (e.g. [speculators](vllm-project/speculat

vllm.title: [Bugfix][DSv3.2] Skip indexer weights for index-cache-skipped layers
vllm.author: frida-andersson
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Follow-up to #45895. With index_topk_freq>1 (or an index_topk_pattern), only some layers build a sparse indexer; the rest are skipped at init via _skip_topk. But the checkpoint still ships indexer.* weights for every layer, so load_weights tries to assign them to params that don't exist on t

vllm.title: [ROCm][Perf][MLA] Add AITER FlashAttention MLA prefill backend (`ROCM_AITER_FA`)
vllm.author: xaguilar-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Adds an AITER-backed MLA prefill backend that calls aiter.flash_attn_varlen_func
directly instead of falling back to the upstream CK-tile FlashAttention path on ROCm.
It dispatches the fast aiter::fmha_fwd_* kernel on gfx950 and, unlike the CK
fallback, needs no V padding (native qk=192 / v=

vllm.title: [GLM5.2 Perf] Replace MOE all-reduce with reduce-scatter
vllm.author: yewentao256
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Origin: full -> all-reduce -> full on every rank -> chunk -> local MoE -> all-gather
Now: full -> reduce-scatter -> local shard -> local MoE -> all-gather if needed
Note that this works for deepseek series as well.
Test
vllm serve zai-org/GLM-5.2-FP8   --kv-cache-dtype fp8_e4m3   --enable-ex

vllm.title: [GLM5] Implement op fusion for GLM5/DSV3.2
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR ports #38595 to the new model definitions for GLM-5 and DeepSeek V3.2.
On 8x GB200 GPUs across 2 nodes with TP8, this PR reaches ~290 tok/s using nvidia/GLM-5.2-NVFP4 with MTP=3.
The current op fusion work includes:

Two large fused kernels for miscellaneous RoPE and normalization ops in att

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Transformers backend: recompute `mm_token_type_ids` per request for M-RoPE
vllm.author: decarpentierg
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
The Transformers backend (model_impl="transformers") passes mm_token_type_ids from the cached multimodal features into model.get_rope_index(...). That tensor is full-sequence (one entry per prompt token), but it is cached per multimodal item, keyed by mm_hash (image content) — not by the req

vllm.title: [GLM5] Fix minor typo
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Fixes a minor attribute name error in #46876

vllm.title: [OCP MX ] Add back emulation to available OCP MX backends list
vllm.author: fxmarty-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Addresses #46142 (comment).
We have a single source of truth regarding which backend supports MXFP4/OCP MX - this PR reverts a previous change the made it compulsory to use --moe-backend emulation in order to dispatch on OCP MXFP4/MXFP6 emulation backend (https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm

vllm.title: [ROCm][CI] Fix `rlhf_async_new_apis` Example On ROCm
vllm.author: micah-wil
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Workaround for a RCCL bug (see ROCm/rocm-systems#5756). The weight-transfer NCCL group fails to initialize (P2P/IPC to the peer GPU) when Ray masks GPUs via ROCR_VISIBLE_DEVICES. Tell Ray to leave GPU visibility untouched so every actor can see all GPUs.
Also, I've removed RAY_EXPERIMENTAL_NOSET_CUD

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Mooncake] Fix Mooncake lookup prefixes with DCP > 1
vllm.author: wzhao18
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Current Mooncake store lookup only iterates over TP and PP ranks and fails to consider DCP and PCP ranks. This results in lookup to return True despite keys for certain ranks are missing.
self._lookup_key_prefixes = tuple(
            tuple(
                PoolKey.build_prefix(db.metadata,

vllm.title: [Model] Add support for openai/privacy-filter
vllm.author: fjosw
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Adds support for openai/privacy-filter (OpenAIPrivacyFilterForTokenClassification): a gpt-oss-style MoE encoder (GQA 14/2, YaRN RoPE, 128 experts top-4, attention sinks) repurposed as a bidirectional token classifier for PII detection. Every layer uses non-causal attention with a banded ±sli

vllm.title: [Spec Decode] Avoid redundant hidden-states gather in draft prefill
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Some EAGLE and MTP draft models reuse the same hidden states for both sampling and the next-step input. In these cases, we do not need to gather the last hidden states twice.
This PR adds a small optimization to skip the redundant torch.gather, avoiding unnecessary overhead in the draft-model path.

vllm.title: Remove boilerplate missed by #46820
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
#46820 missed a couple of references to self.expert_weights and this caused models like Gemma4 Unified to atempt to read a non-existent attribute from the language model.
This PR removes the remaining references to self.expert_weights in all modelling code.
Fixes #46948

vllm.title: [EPLB] Mask padding in EPLB load recording
vllm.author: ilmarkov
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
When CUDA graphs or DP padding inflate the batch size, padded tokens flow through the MoE router and get counted in eplb_map_to_physical_and_record, polluting the expert load statistics used for EPLB rebalancing.
This PR adds a num_unpadded_tokens and num_unpadded_tokens_tensor field to Eplb

vllm.title: [CPU] Support cpu compressed-tensor w8a8 int8 moe
vllm.author: yuwenzho
vllm.bodyExcerpt:

Support cpu compressed-tensor w8a8 int8 moe

python -m pytest tests/kernels/moe/test_cpu_quant_fused_moe.py -v

Add a native Harmony chat renderer for GPT-OSS in the Rust frontend.
